title: dalechampia canescens subsp. friedrichthalii
intro: Dalechampia canescens subsp. friedrichthalii, a sub-species of the Dalechampia canescens plant, is a species of flowering plant in the Euphorbiaceae family, commonly known as the fire vine. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Dalechampia canescens subsp. friedrichthalii include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water, and are used to treat a variety of health issues in traditional medicine.
